lesson.title: Multiplying Polynomials (FOIL)
lesson.body: To multiply two binomials, use **FOIL**: **F**irst, **O**uter, **I**nner, **L**ast. Example: (x + 3)(x âˆ’ 5) = xÂ² âˆ’ 5x + 3x âˆ’ 15 = xÂ² âˆ’ 2x âˆ’ 15. For larger polynomials, distribute each term of the first polynomial across every term of the second. Special patterns: **(a + b)Â² = aÂ² + 2ab + bÂ²** (perfect square), **(a âˆ’ b)Â² = aÂ² âˆ’ 2ab + bÂ²**, **(a + b)(a âˆ’ b) = aÂ² âˆ’ bÂ²** (difference of squares).

rooms.question: Expand: (x + 4)(x + 3)
rooms.explanation: FOIL: First: xÂ·x=xÂ². Outer: xÂ·3=3x. Inner: 4Â·x=4x. Last: 4Â·3=12. Combine: xÂ²+(3x+4x)+12 = xÂ²+7x+12. The xÂ²+12x+7 error adds 3+4=7 for the constant and multiplies 3Ã—4=12 for the x term â€” swapping the middle and last steps.

rooms.question: Expand: (2x âˆ’ 3)(x + 5)
rooms.explanation: FOIL: 2xÂ·x=2xÂ²; 2xÂ·5=10x; âˆ’3Â·x=âˆ’3x; âˆ’3Â·5=âˆ’15. Combine: 2xÂ²+(10xâˆ’3x)âˆ’15 = 2xÂ²+7xâˆ’15. The 2xÂ²âˆ’7xâˆ’15 error reverses the x signs, computing 10xâˆ’3x as âˆ’7x.

rooms.question: Expand: (x + 5)Â²
rooms.explanation: (x+5)Â² = xÂ² + 2(x)(5) + 5Â² = xÂ² + 10x + 25. The xÂ²+25 error is the most common mistake â€” squaring only the first and last terms, forgetting the middle term 2ab.

rooms.question: Expand: (3x âˆ’ 2)(3x + 2)
rooms.explanation: Difference of squares: (aâˆ’b)(a+b) = aÂ²âˆ’bÂ². Here a=3x, b=2. Result: 9xÂ²âˆ’4. No middle term! The outer and inner terms cancel: +6xâˆ’6x=0. The 9xÂ²âˆ’12xâˆ’4 error uses FOIL without recognizing the pattern.

rooms.question: Put the FOIL steps in order for: (2x + 1)(x âˆ’ 4)
items: Last: 1 Â· (âˆ’4) = âˆ’4; combine: 2xÂ² âˆ’ 7x âˆ’ 4
rooms.explanation: FOIL in order: First (xÂ·x terms), Outer (first Ã— last), Inner (last Ã— first), Last (lastÃ—last). Then combine like terms: âˆ’8x+x = âˆ’7x. Final: 2xÂ²âˆ’7xâˆ’4.

boss.name: The FOIL Specter
boss.taunt: First. Outer. Inner. Last. Then darkness.

questions.question: Expand: (x + 2)(xÂ² âˆ’ 3x + 4)
questions.explanation: Distribute x: x(xÂ²âˆ’3x+4) = xÂ³âˆ’3xÂ²+4x. Distribute 2: 2(xÂ²âˆ’3x+4) = 2xÂ²âˆ’6x+8. Sum: xÂ³+(âˆ’3+2)xÂ²+(4âˆ’6)x+8 = xÂ³âˆ’xÂ²âˆ’2x+8. The last option error only distributes x, forgetting to distribute 2.

questions.question: What is (x âˆ’ 3)Â²?
questions.explanation: (aâˆ’b)Â² = aÂ²âˆ’2ab+bÂ². Here a=x, b=3: xÂ²âˆ’2(x)(3)+9 = xÂ²âˆ’6x+9. The xÂ²âˆ’9 error only squares each term. The xÂ²âˆ’3x+9 error uses 1Ã—coefficient instead of 2Ã—coefficient for the middle term.

questions.question: Expand and simplify: (x + 2)Â² âˆ’ (x âˆ’ 2)Â²
questions.explanation: (x+2)Â² = xÂ²+4x+4. (xâˆ’2)Â² = xÂ²âˆ’4x+4. Subtract: (xÂ²+4x+4)âˆ’(xÂ²âˆ’4x+4) = 8x. The xÂ² and constant terms cancel. This is a difference of squares pattern: (x+2)Â²âˆ’(xâˆ’2)Â² = [(x+2)+(xâˆ’2)][(x+2)âˆ’(xâˆ’2)] = (2x)(4) = 8x.
